name: Scott LaFaro
abstract: Rocco Scott LaFaro (April 3, 1936 – July 6, 1961) was an American jazz double bassist known for his work with the Bill Evans Trio. LaFaro broke new ground on the instrument, developing a countermelodic style of accompaniment rather than playing traditional walking basslines, as well as virtuosity that was practically unmatched by any of …

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Morell
about_quotes.evidence: I started to listen to Bill, a lot, the early trio with Scott LaFaro and Paul Motian... So it was my favorite jazz group at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Morell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: I just wanted to ask you about, just a little bit about your time with Bill Evans... This was written about Scott LaFaro... you didn’t feel any pressure to like try to recreate what he was doing? ... I was playing music like that... Scotty wasn’t alone... I was playing music like that
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: If my bass hadn't of broke, I'd of sounded a lot more like Scott Lafaro than Scott Lafaro did.

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Marino
about_quotes.evidence: stories I’ve heard, I wasn’t there you know, I never got to see him play live
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Marino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 25, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: he had the incredible brief good fortune of involving himself with Scott LaFaro... I had recorded, I’d done two studio recordings with that trio... we set that up and actually the way it worked out we knew we wanted to do a Sunday... So we go in there on what turns out to be the last day of the gig.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Gene was looking for a gig. So he came up. And so here’s all of these young college guys and this one guy who’d been on the road with everybody and lived with Scott LaFaro and lived with Joe Romano.
